summary: Implemented four grouped tasks that all touch Dashboard.tsx. Task 2.4: created SpeciesAvatar component with inline SVG silhouettes for dog, cat, bird, rabbit, hamster, fish, reptile, and other (paw print); replaced the generic person-SVG fallback in Dashboard.tsx and the emoji fallback in PhotoUpload.tsx (both compact and non-compact modes). Task 7.1: removed 'View profile' badge from pet card footer, enlarged chevron from 16px to 24px, added hover:shadow-lg transition-shadow on the Link card, and changed the footer row from justify-between to justify-end since only the chevron remains. Task 6.2 frontend: added user_role?: string to the Pet interface in client.ts; in Dashboard.tsx renders a 'Shared with you' badge next to the pet name when user_role is 'editor' or 'viewer'. Task 6.3: added EmptyStateContent interface and 'empty_state' to BlockType union in cms.ts; Dashboard.tsx fetches the 'dashboard-empty-state' CMS page when pets.length === 0, extracts the empty_state block, and uses its heading/subheading — with silent fallback to hardcoded defaults on CMS failure.
notes_for_reviewer: PhotoUpload non-compact mode did not receive a species prop previously; it now falls back to 'other' (paw print) when species is undefined, matching the old generic paw emoji behavior.
The 'Shared with you' badge reuses the existing badge-navy class to stay consistent with the removed 'View profile' badge styling.
CMS fetch for empty state is fire-and-forget inside loadPets — it does not block the loading spinner or cause a second render cycle if pets array is non-empty.
